WSJ Technology.summary: Elon Musk’s lawyers said they would appeal the verdict. The jury absolved Musk of “engaging in a scheme to defraud Twitter investors.”

WSJ Technology.title: ‘New Humans: Memories of the Future’ Review: The New Museum’s Maximalist New Chapter
WSJ Technology.summary: Reopening after a high-profile expansion, the institution stages a sprawling and enthralling inaugural show that examines how technology shapes ideas of what it means to be human.

WSJ Economy.title: Heard on the Street: Investors with a stomach for political risk can get 30% off the price of U.S. homes right now
WSJ Economy.summary: A bill that would limit the activities of big housing investors has turned Wall Street landlords into a value play.

WSJ Economy.title: Americans are spending more than ever on manicures and doggy daycare
WSJ Economy.summary: Consumers have plenty of cash to pay for all kinds of services, but rising prices are feeding into sticky inflation.

WSJ Economy.title: Polish Inflation Holding Near Target Despite Iran War, Gov. Glapinski Says
WSJ Economy.summary: EXCLUSIVE: As investors jettisoned expectations for rate cuts in Europe following the outbreak of war in Iran, the National Bank of Poland lowered borrowing costs, underscoring its confidence inflation will remain contained.

WSJ Economy.title: Japan Companies Signal Biggest Wage Hike in 35 Years
WSJ Economy.summary: Preliminary data from the nation’s largest labor union group showed that 1,100 members secured wage increases of 5.26% on average this year.

WSJ Economy.title: Kevin Warsh promised to overhaul the Federal Reserve and cut rates. He is being greeted by rising inflation, an oil shock and a confirmation in limbo.
WSJ Economy.summary: Kevin Warsh promised to overhaul the central bank and cut rates. He is being greeted by rising inflation, an oil shock and a confirmation in limbo.

WSJ Economy.title: Small truck drivers in the U.S. are feeling some of the first economic effects of the rapid surge in the cost of diesel, and a broader economic impact could hit soon
WSJ Economy.summary: Truck drivers in the U.S. are feeling some of the first economic effects of the rapid surge in the cost of diesel. A broader economic impact could hit soon.

WSJ Economy.title: More would-be home sellers are renting out their houses and condos, unwilling to cut prices in a sluggish housing market
WSJ Economy.summary: About 2.2% of rental listings on Zillow in November had previously been listed for sale, according to a Zillow analysis, the highest level since late 2022 when mortgage rates surged.

WSJ Economy.title: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung has nominated Shin Hyun-song, a senior Bank for International Settlements official, as the country’s next central bank governor
WSJ Economy.summary: Shin Hyun-song will replace departing Bank of Korea Governor Rhee Chang-yong, whose four-year term ends next month.

WSJ Economy.title: A Fed rate increase, once unthinkable, has become thinkable thanks to stubborn inflation, Iran and a resilient economy, @greg_ip writes
WSJ Economy.summary: A rate increase, once unthinkable, has become thinkable thanks to stubborn inflation, Iran and a resilient economy.

WSJ Economy.title: Young people, who are just starting their careers, are trying to figure out which professions will insulate them from AI’s impact—or what jobs will benefit from it
WSJ Economy.summary: They’ve got their whole careers ahead of them, and they’re navigating a technology with a still-uncertain impact.
